subject: matched OperatorBench context and lexical-retrieval comparison
paired_observations: structured and raw contexts have identical fixed-proposal success while structured uses 397.83 fewer mean characters; the paired 95 percent bootstrap interval is [-445.5, -327.0] characters
structured exceeds latest-N fixed-proposal success by 0.8333; the paired 95 percent bootstrap interval is [0.5, 1.0]
FTS5 exceeds deterministic term retrieval fixed-proposal success by 0.1667, but its paired 95 percent bootstrap interval [-0.3333, 0.6667] crosses zero
FTS5 uses 58 more mean characters than deterministic term retrieval; its paired 95 percent bootstrap interval is [1.8333, 165.0] characters
recorded latency is deliberately zeroed for reproducibility and supplies no latency comparison
reason: The matched experiment, artifact, and uncertainty contracts are reproducible, but six synthetic scenarios with replayed fixed proposals cannot estimate a model-dependent context effect. The lexical retrieval difference is uncertain and FTS5 consumes more context in this fixture set.
promotion_prerequisites: expand to at least 20 preregistered scenarios spanning chronology and scenario families
run at least three paired replicates for each stochastic model condition
pin one explicit model identifier, model configuration, action schema, context ceiling, retrieval cap, and grader version across treatments
retain the canonical live report before provider calls and report non-zero end-to-end latency, token usage, and provider cost when available
promote an intervention only when paired uncertainty supports improved success or materially smaller context at non-inferior success
